text:
Deep in the forest, an ancient tree whispered secrets to any who listened. The wise old tree had seen every moon rise and every shade of autumn's gold.

One bright morning, a young squirrel named Benny chattered excitedly to the tree about a glittering treasure hidden somewhere under its great branches. "Every night," Benny promised, "a golden moth flies out, singing a song so enchanting, it makes me forget my home."

Curiosity piqued, Benny embarked on a perilous quest. Day after day, he'd creep close to the shimmering moths but retreat at their song. Each night, he imagined it closer, hearing laughter like fairy melodies.

After many nights, Benny dared to reach out and touch the gold. His paws brushed something soft and warm. It was a map! He skimmed it, curious.

"Seek the hollow bark where moonbeams dance," it read. "There, you'll find the sapstone of fate." Benny remembered this
